chunks.page_summary: This document describes how to integrate Rheonics sensors with a USR-W610 Wi-Fi and Ethernet Converter to transmit sensor data wirelessly over a Wi-Fi network, enabling remote monitoring and visualization.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Connecting Rheonics Sensors to Wi-Fi Networks using USR-W610
Modified on: Mon, 20 Oct, 2025 at 6:38 PM

What is the purpose of this article?
This article demonstrates how to integrate the USR-W610 RS232/RS485 to Wi-Fi and Ethernet Converter with Rheonics sensors to transmit data over a Wi-Fi network and access it remotely.

What products are involved?
This article is based on using the Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME) (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/) and the USR-W610 RS232/RS485 to Wi-Fi and Ethernet Converter.

TABLE OF CONTENTS
1. Overview
2. Prerequisites
3. USR-W610 Setup through built-in Web server
  3.1. USR-W610 Configuration
  3.2. Modbus RTU parameters configuration
4. Accessing Rheonics SME Data via the USR-W610
  4.1. Wiring the USR-W610 to the Rheonics SME
  4.2. Reading Sensor Parameters Using Modbus Poll
5. Resources

1. Overview
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005)
To enable wireless data access, Rheonics offers the USR-W610 RS232/RS485 to Wi-Fi and Ethernet Converter as an option. The USR-W610 transmits Modbus RTU data from the Sensor Module Electronics (SME) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005) over a Wi-Fi network, enabling wireless communication and remote access. This article describes how to integrate a Rheonics sensor with the USR-W610 to transmit sensor data over Wi-Fi for remote monitoring and visualization.

The USR-W610 is provided in its default Access Point (AP) mode, which allows connecting to the module wirelessly and performing the corresponding configurations to access a Wi-Fi network. However, station (STA) mode is used to read the sensor's data and connect to a Wi-Fi network, as shown in the following diagram.

Figure 1: Rheonics Sensor with the USR-W610 connected to an Access Point

2. Prerequisites
Rheonics SME (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/) with Modbus RTU (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005) enabled
USR-W610 RS232/RS485 To Wi-Fi and Ethernet Converter

3. USR-W610 Setup through built-in Web server
There are three possible ways of configuring the USR-W610, as AP, STA and as AP+STA.
Access Point (AP): In this case, the USR-W610 acts similar to a router, which means other devices can connect to it.
STA (station): In this configuration, the USR-W610 connects to an existing access point like a router allowing the device to access internet.
AP + STA : Combines both modes.

In this article, STA configuration is explained to receive data from Rheonics SME (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/) through its serial port.

3.1. USR-W610 Configuration
The USR-W610 is provided in Access Point (AP) mode by default, creating its own Wi-Fi network that other devices (laptops, tablets, others) can connect to. In AP mode, the corresponding configurations are made to use STA mode, so the USR-W610 connects to an existing Wi-Fi network and transmits Rheonics sensor data through it. Other devices connected to the same Wi-Fi network can visualize the sensor data as seen in the figure.
key_facts: The article demonstrates how to integrate the USR-W610 RS232/RS485 to Wi-Fi and Ethernet Converter with Rheonics sensors.
The USR-W610 facilitates wireless data access from Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME) by transmitting Modbus RTU data over Wi-Fi.
The USR-W610 operates in Access Point (AP) mode by default, but Station (STA) mode is used to connect to an existing Wi-Fi network and read sensor data.
Three configuration modes for the USR-W610 are available: Access Point (AP), STA (station), and AP+STA.
This article specifically explains STA configuration for receiving data from Rheonics SME via its serial port.
diagram_labels: Rheonics Sensors (eg. SRV, SRD, DVP, others) for density, viscosity, temperature

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for connecting the USR-W610 device to a Wi-Fi network and accessing its web server for configuration within a Rheonics system.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 2: Data transmission of the USR-W610 through Wi-Fi
1. After powering the USR-W610, open the available Wi-Fi networks and connect to Rheonics Network.
Figure 3: Rheonics Network
2. The Link LED should light up after a successful network connection.
Figure 4: Link LED on after Wi-Fi connection
3. Access the web server by entering the default IP address in a browser and the credentials provided on the device label.
  * IP: 10.10.100.254 (default)
  * User: admin
  * Password: admin
key_facts: The USR-W610 is an RS232/485 to WiFi and Ethernet Converter.
The USR-W610 can operate as a Station (STA) in a Wi-Fi network.
Data transfer occurs via Wi-Fi from the USR-W610 to an Access Point, enabling remote visualization.
The USR-W610 can communicate with a Rheonics SME device via RS485.
To establish an initial connection, users must connect to the 'Rheonics Network' Wi-Fi.
A successful network connection is indicated by the Link LED illuminating on the USR-W610 device.
The device's web server is accessible via the default IP address 10.10.100.254.
The default credentials for accessing the web server are 'admin' for both username and password.
diagram_labels: RS232/485 To WiFi and Ethernet Converter

key_facts: The document provides instructions for configuring the USR-W610 Serial Device Server.
The default IP address for accessing the device's web server is 10.10.100.254, with default login credentials admin/admin.
Wi-Fi network configuration is performed under the 'STA Interface Setting' menu option in the web interface.
Users can search for available Wi-Fi networks to connect the device.
The example configuration uses WPA2PSK security mode with AES encryption.
Changes to network settings must be applied using the 'Apply' button.
The device label shows compliance marks: CE, FC, EAC, RoHS.

key_facts: The device's web interface, identified as an M2M Web Server, is accessible via the IP address 10.10.100.254.
A Site Survey feature within the web interface displays available Wi-Fi networks with details such as SSID, BSSID, RSSI, Channel, Encryption, Authentication, and Network Type.
To connect the device to a Wi-Fi network in STA (Station) mode, the user must enter the network's 'Pass Phrase' (password) in the 'STA Interface Setting' section.
The default configuration for the STA interface includes WPA2PSK as the Security Mode and AES as the Encryption Type.
The device's network settings indicate a WAN Connection Type of 'DHCP (Auto config)' and a default hostname of 'USR-W610_3944'.
All configuration changes made through the web interface require the device to be restarted via the 'Device Management' section to be saved and applied.
diagram_labels: You could configure STA interface parameters and turn on/off AP+STA here.
Set Successfully, Restart to use new setting.
warnings_notes: A 'Not secure' indicator is present in the browser's address bar, indicating that the connection to the M2M Web Server is not encrypted.

After initiating a restart, a 'rebooting' message will appear, requiring the user to wait a few seconds before continuing.
To configure a fixed IP address, the WAN Connection Type must be set to 'STATIC (fixed IP)' within the STA Interface Setting.
The chosen fixed IP address must be within the same IP range as the Wi-Fi network to which the device is connecting.
The example configuration uses 192.168.100.73 as the fixed IP address based on the Wi-Fi network properties, with a subnet mask of 255.255.255.0 and a default gateway of 192.168.100.1.

The device can operate in either AP (Access Point) Mode or STA (Station Mode).
The data transfer mode can be set to Modbus TCP <-> Modbus RTU.
A successful Wi-Fi connection on the USR-W610 is indicated by the illumination of the Link LED.
Access to the web server for configuration requires an IP address and default credentials.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a step-by-step guide for configuring Modbus RTU serial communication parameters on the USR-W610 device through its web interface, referencing Rheonics guidelines.
chunks.extracted_text:
3.2. Modbus RTU parameters configuration

To configure the serial port parameters of the Rheonics SME (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/), follow the next steps.

1. Enter the USR-W610 and select Application Setting.

Figure 18: Remote access to the configuration page using STA
[Web Interface - Left Navigation]
  Quick Configure
  Mode Selection
  AP Interface Setting
  STA Interface Setting
  Application Setting
  Ethernet Setting
  HTTPD Client Mode
  Advanced
  Device Management

[Web Interface - Main Content, Quick Configure]
  1F Wi-Fi Setting [Modify]
    Wifi Mode: Mode
    STA Interface Parameters:
      AP's SSID
      MAC Address (Optional)
      Security Mode
      Encryption Type
      Key
  2F Ethernet Ports Setting [Modify]
  3F Uart Setting [Modify]
  4F Network Setting [Modify]
  5F Device Management
  Restart Module: Restart

Figure 19: Application Setting section
[Web Interface - Left Navigation with "Application Setting" highlighted]

2. Configure Modbus RTU parameters according to Rheonics guideline in Connecting Modbus RTU (RS-485) outputs (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297458-connecting-modbus-rtu-rs-485-outputs).
  * Baudrate: 38400
  * Data Bits: 8
  * Parity: Odd
  * Stop: 1
  * Flow control: Disable
  * 485 mode: Enable
  * Baudrate adaptive (RFC2117): Enable

Figure 20: Serial communication parameters
[Web Interface - Wifi-Uart Setting]
  Wifi-Uart Setting
  You could configure the Uart parameters and network parameters of the wifi-uart application.
  Uart Setting:
    Baudrate: [Dropdown: 38400]
    Data Bits: [Dropdown: 8]
    Parity: [Dropdown: Odd]
    Stop: [Dropdown: 1]
    Flow control: [Dropdown: Disable]
    485 mode: [Dropdown: Enable]
    Baudrate adaptive (RFC2117): [Dropdown: Enable]
  [Button: Apply] [Button: Cancel]

3. Under Network A Setting, configure as follows and click on Apply.
key_facts: The USR-W610 device's serial port parameters can be configured through its web interface.
The configuration process for Modbus RTU parameters begins by selecting 'Application Setting' on the device's configuration page.
Specific parameters such as Baudrate, Data Bits, Parity, Stop, Flow control, 485 mode, and Baudrate adaptive are defined for Modbus RTU configuration.
Rheonics provides a guideline for connecting Modbus RTU (RS-485) outputs, which should be followed during configuration.
The 'Wifi-Uart Setting' section is used to configure Uart and network parameters for the wifi-uart application.
Configuration changes must be saved by clicking the 'Apply' button.
diagram_labels: Figure 18: Remote access to the configuration page using STA

4. Accessing Rheonics SME Data via the USR-W610
Once the USR-W610 configuration is complete, the Rheonics sensor data can be accessed wirelessly over Wi-Fi.

4.1. Wiring the USR-W610 to the Rheonics SME
Wire the SME (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/) to the USR-W610 serial port as shown in the diagram.

Figure 22: Wiring diagram of USR-W610 to Rheonics SME

4.2. Reading Sensor Parameters Using Modbus Poll
1. To read data using Modbus Poll, open the Modbus Poll software.

Figure 23: Modbus Poll software

2. Click on the Connection Tab and select Connect.
key_facts: The USR-W610 is an RS232/485 to WiFi and Ethernet Converter.
Rheonics sensor data can be accessed wirelessly over Wi-Fi after the USR-W610 is configured.
The SME refers to Rheonics electronics and communication devices, which connect to the USR-W610 serial port.
Modbus Poll software is used to read parameters from Rheonics sensors.
Rheonics sensors measure density, viscosity, and temperature.
The USR-W610 can operate in Server mode with TCP protocol.
diagram_labels: PUSR USR-W610 RS232/485 To Wifi and Ethernet Converter
Rheonics Sensors (eg. SRV, SRD, DVP, others) for density, viscosity, temperature
RX = 0, TX = 0, ID = 1, F = 03, SR = 1000ms, No connection (status bar)

4. Sensor data is available in the input registers. (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297459-modbus-input-registers) The table below shows the commonly used registers.

Parameter 12 Viscosity Last Good 136 300137 2 Float32 Parameter 12 value as float
Parameter 13 Density Last Good 144 300145 2 Float32 Parameter 13 value as float
Parameter 2 Temperature median 56 300057 2 Float32 Parameter 2 value as float

5. Click on Setup and Read/Write Definition.

Figure 26: Setup Tab

6. Configure the following parameters to read Viscosity Last Good and click OK.

*   Slave ID: 1 (default)
*   Function: 04 Read Input Registers (3x)
*   Address: 136
*   Quantity: 2
key_facts: Modbus RTU communication can be converted to Modbus TCP through setup configuration.
The default port for Modbus TCP/IP server is 502.
Sensor data like viscosity, density, and temperature are available in Modbus input registers.
Viscosity Last Good is found at Modbus address 136, Density Last Good at 144, and Temperature Median at 56, each requiring 2 registers (Float32).
Specific Modbus parameters for reading 'Viscosity Last Good' are Slave ID 1, Function 04 Read Input Registers (3x), Address 136, and Quantity 2.
diagram_labels: You could configure STA Interface parameters and turn on/off AP+STA here.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions and resources for configuring Modbus communication using a 'Read/Write Definition' interface and visualizing input registers with 'Modbus Poll' software.
chunks.extracted_text:
Read/Write Definition
Slave ID: 1
Function: 04 Read Input Registers (3x)
Address: 136
Protocol address: E.g. 30011 -> 10
Quantity: 2

Take into consideration that the SME (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005) slave ID remains the same when Modbus RTU (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297635-modbus-tcp-protocol) is converted to Modbus TCP (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297635-modbus-tcp-protocol). Ensure that the slave ID matches the value configured in your SME (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/) settings.

7. Configure any additional input registers as required. The corresponding values should now be displayed correctly.

key_facts: The SME slave ID remains constant when Modbus RTU is converted to Modbus TCP.
The slave ID configured in the Modbus 'Read/Write Definition' must match the value set in the SME settings.
Additional input registers can be configured as required, after which their corresponding values should display correctly.
The document provides links to Rheonics support resources for Modbus communication topics, including Modbus RTU, Modbus TCP, and input registers.
A USR-W610 RS232/485 to Wi-Fi/Ethernet Converter is listed as a resource, with links to its manual and product page.
warnings_notes: Take into consideration that the SME slave ID remains the same when Modbus RTU is converted to Modbus TCP.
Ensure that the slave ID matches the value configured in your SME settings.
